title: STANDARD FOR WHEY POWDERS CXS 289-1995 Formerly CODEX STAN A-15-1995. Adopted in 1995. Revised in 2003. Amended in 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR WHEY POWDERS CXS 289-1995 Formerly CODEX STAN A-15-1995. Adopted in 1995. Revised in 2003. Amended in 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.
sections.summary: Title and adoption/revision history for the Codex Standard for Whey Powders (CXS 289-1995), noting adoption in 1995 and subsequent revisions/amendments through 2022.

Notes a 2022 amendment adopted at the 45th CAC that replaces the previous non‑retail container labelling provision with a requirement to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
This standard applies to Whey Powder and Acid Whey Powder intended for direct consumption or further processing as described in Section 2.

Defines whey powders as milk products obtained by drying whey or acid whey, with 'whey' produced by rennet-type coagulation during cheese/casein manufacture and 'acid whey' produced principally by acidification.

Sets essential composition and quality factors including raw material (whey/acid whey), permitted ingredient (seed lactose for pre‑crystallized powder), and specific compositional criteria and limits for whey powder and acid whey powder (e.g., reference lactose, minimum protein, maximum water and ash, pH/titratable acidity) with calculation notes and allowance for compositional modifications per the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999).
Specifies the raw material for the standard as whey or acid whey.
Permits the use of seed lactose in the manufacture of pre‑crystallized whey powder.

Provides detailed compositional tables for whey powder and acid whey powder (including lactose reference value, minimum protein levels, maximum water and ash, and pH/titratable acidity criteria) plus notes on expression of lactose, protein calculation, water exclusions, acidity equivalence and allowance for compositional modification to meet end‑product composition under CXS 206-1999.

Specifies composition criteria particular to acid whey powder (reference lactose 61.0% m/m, minimum protein 7.0% m/m, maximum water 4.5% m/m, maximum ash 15.0% m/m, and pH/titratable acidity limits) with accompanying calculation and modification notes.
Permits use of food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for Food Category 01.8.2 (dried whey and whey products, excluding whey cheese).

Requires the products and the milk used in their manufacture to comply with maximum levels for contaminants and toxins in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with CAC-established maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides in milk.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts, and compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Sets specific labelling provisions requiring the name 'Whey powder' or 'Acid whey powder', obliges a prominent qualification when fat or lactose contents differ from reference levels, allows the term 'sweet' only if specified compositional criteria are met (see Section 7.1), and requires non‑retail container labelling to follow CXS 346-2021.

Declares the names 'Whey powder' and 'Acid whey powder', requires that products with fat and/or lactose contents outside the reference levels be accompanied by a clear qualification, and permits the qualifying term 'sweet' only when compositional thresholds are met (minimum lactose 65%, minimum protein 11%, maximum ash 8.5%, and pH > 6 or titratable acidity ≤ 0.16%).
Requires labelling of non-retail food containers to comply with the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers (CXS 346-2021).

Specifies that methods of sampling and analysis for checking compliance with this standard must use the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions.
